briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate change expert spokesperson briefing for July 5, 2026. Based on the key climate-related news collected today, we will provide a comprehensive report on the severity of the current climate crisis and domestic and international efforts to address it.

sections.title: 🔴 Urgent Climate Crisis Warning and Spreading Economic Impacts
sections.content: The current climate crisis is causing unprecedented heatwaves globally, leading to severe economic losses. Japan's summer has already transformed into Southeast Asia-level heat, resulting in an annual loss of 2.9 billion working hours [1, 9, 10, 16, 26]. In Europe, disaster-level heatwaves are also being observed, with 'hot nights' increasing 100-fold [25]. These physical risks are spreading to the financial system, with the Financial Services Commission pushing for a policy requiring companies to specify the impact of climate change on their sales in business reports, causing alarm in the business community [6, 14, 20]. Some companies, like LX International, are quantifying the 'cost' of climate change and accelerating their structural transformation [2, 13]. Traditional industries, such as seaweed farming off the coast of Gunsan, are undertaking direct adaptation efforts by choosing alternative seafood products to cope with climate change [7].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Climate Change Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Domestically, concrete adaptation efforts are actively underway to respond to the direct threats of climate change. Geumcheon-gu, Seoul, has completed the creation of 'Climate Change Safe Parks' that citizens can use with peace of mind even during heatwaves and cold snaps, enhancing the city's climate resilience [3, 4, 5, 15, 17, 18, 19, 22, 23, 27, 28, 30]. Furthermore, Kakao and the Korea Meteorological Administration have agreed to strengthen cooperation to respond to hazardous weather, such as heatwaves caused by climate change, through a business agreement [8, 12]. The National Arboretum is contributing to climate change research by releasing 17 years of accumulated plant phenology observation data [29]. Efforts to achieve a carbon-neutral society are also being made in various ways, with cooperation between the Chungnam Sustainable Development Council and the Korea Low-Carbon Diet Association [31], and the participation of religious communities expanding, including the Buddhist community launching a Carbon Neutrality Practice Group [32, 36]. Yongin City is accelerating carbon neutrality by supporting electric cargo vehicle subsidies [33], and various local governments, including Gwacheon City and Danyang County, are promoting community participation by operating carbon neutrality support centers and initiating environmental education [34, 35, 37]. Rural women in Gyeongju have also pledged to innovate agriculture and practice carbon neutrality [38], and Namdong Power is expanding carbon neutrality technology exchange through a special lecture on CO₂ capture and conversion technology [39].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Presenting New Models
sections.content: Globally, the transition to renewable energy is accelerating, accompanied by economic benefits. The International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) reported that renewable energy has saved 742 trillion Korean Won (approximately 530 billion USD) in fossil fuel costs worldwide, with an additional 480 billion USD in cost savings expected by 2025 [40, 42]. Domestically, the basic plan for renewable energy and the electricity demand outlook have been announced, setting the direction for energy transition [41]. The Minister of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs visited Sinan County to inspect a new basic income model for farming and fishing villages utilizing renewable energy profits and the progress of solar power projects [43, 44, 45, 48]. Sinan County's solar-powered basic income is drawing attention for its positive impact on population rebound [50]. In Hwaseong City, the 'No. 1 Hwaseong-type Basic Income Energy Village' has begun operation as the first fruit of resident-led renewable energy policy, presenting a self-reliant energy transition model for the local community [47]. Furthermore, corporate efforts to transition to renewable energy are actively continuing, with Samsung Vietnam operating a 28MWp solar rooftop power plant, and overall, companies are accelerating their eco-friendly transition to renewable energy [46, 49].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community Confronts Climate Crisis and Seeks Diverse Responses
sections.content: The international community is facing various threats due to climate change, leading to ongoing concerns and the search for solutions. A village in Devon, UK, is facing an 'existential' threat due to climate change [52, 55], and an analysis in Hawaii suggests that coral reef recreational losses could incur economic costs of up to 3 billion USD [53]. Climate change particularly threatens the health of vulnerable populations [54], and an invisible link to cervical cancer is even being raised [58]. It has also been pointed out that the challenges of housing adaptation cannot be met with past tools and require a complete overhaul [57]. Amidst this sense of crisis, a survey indicates that two-thirds of the public are concerned about climate change [56], and the voice of civil society is growing, with Greenpeace warning about the severity of the climate crisis through a campaign to rename London Underground stations [24]. Meanwhile, some studies have also presented the positive possibility that extensive coral reef areas could be resilient to climate change [59].

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, climate change is no longer a future threat but an ongoing reality, demanding urgent and multifaceted responses on a global scale. Governments, businesses, and civil society worldwide must cooperate to overcome the climate crisis and build a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: U.S. 250th Anniversary Marred by Division, 'Privatized' by Trump Amidst Severe Weather and Delayed Guinness-Level Fireworks
articles.original_title: 악천후에도 “연설 강행” 자정 돼서야 기네스급 불꽃놀이···트럼프가 ‘사유화’한 미국 건국 250주년, 분열로 얼룩져
articles.description: On July 4th (local time), to mark the 250th anniversary of the U.S. founding, 860,000 fireworks were launched at the National Mall in Washington D.C. The skies of Washington D.C. were illuminated by 860,000 fireworks, commemorating the nation's 250th anniversary. This Independence Day was planned to be exceptionally spectacular, featuring a massive fireworks display that broke a Guinness World Record and a grand air show. However, it was held amidst scorching heat and thunderstorms...

summary.phenomenon: On July 4th (local time), 860,000 fireworks were launched at the National Mall in Washington D.C. to commemorate the 250th anniversary of the U.S. founding. This Guinness World Record-breaking fireworks display occurred amidst scorching heat and thunderstorms.
summary.cause: Not mentioned in the article. (The article notes the presence of scorching heat and thunderstorms but does not specify the scientific causes of these weather phenomena.)

articles.title: From ‘heat panic’ to ‘sacrificed at the altar’: Europe’s air conditioning culture wars heat up
articles.description: Cooling down has become political amid record highs, as experts say row is distracting from work of protecting livesAs the afternoon heat rose to a dizzying 41.7C (107F) in eastern Brandenburg on Sunday, taking German temperatures to unprecedented highs, Mario, 65, took precautions but did not panic. Two years ago, a fierce heatwave had prompted him to buy a powerful device that few Germans own: an air conditioning unit.“The summers are slowly getting warmer,” says the retired handyman in Neuzel
